mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses near-term equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during months with rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate at or above 5.0 percent signals sufficient labor market slack to suppress wage-driven consumption and compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of instances when UNRATE is at or above 5.0%, or the t-statistic of the return difference relative to baseline is not negative at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated contraction in both final demand and manufacturing output, which amplifies recession risk beyond either signal alone and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO has a t-statistic greater than -1.5 in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint decline condition fails to produce negative returns in >50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of restrictive monetary policy defined by a federal funds rate above 3.5 percent, accelerating consumer prices force the central bank to maintain tight conditions, elevating real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales relative to its prior month level signals weakening household consumption expenditure, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The month-over-month decline in RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC more negative than -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth coincides with a rising federal funds rate, the positive labor signal is offset by tightening financial conditions, resulting in net negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as discount rate effects dominate cash flow effects.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS change and FEDFUNDS level is not significantly negative (t-stat > -1.5) in predicting 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 2100 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment and construction employment, which transmits to broader economic weakness and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 2100.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production over a three-month horizon signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns do not occur in >50% of quarters with rising INDPRO.
